records.ety: [LL. commissarius, fr. L. commissus, p. p. of committere to commit, intrust to. See Commit.]

defs: One to whom is committed some charge, duty, or office, by a superior power; a commissioner.
quotes: Great Destiny, the Commissary of God.

defs: An officer of the bishop, who exercises ecclesiastical jurisdiction in parts of the diocese at a distance from the residence of the bishop.
An officer having charge of a special service; as, the commissary of musters.
An officer whose business is to provide food for a body of troops or a military post; -- officially called commissary of subsistence.
quotes: Washington wrote to the President of Congress . . . urging the appointment of a commissary general, a quartermaster general, a commissary of musters, and a commissary of artillery.
